meta.title: logy

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: logy
root.mentor_note: logy is the quiet root behind nearly every science’s name, tucked at the end rather than the front.

attempt.direction_prompt: A casual hobby, or a formal field of scientific study?

context_sentences: A single dissected frog in year nine biology was enough to convince her medicine was not for her.
His biology teacher kept a wall of preserved specimens that every student remembered decades later.
The dig site had drawn archaeology students from four different universities that summer.
A career in archaeology, she quickly learned, involved far more paperwork than actual digging.
The company had once used graphology to screen job applicants, a practice it quietly dropped years ago.
Graphology remains popular in a few countries despite little evidence it reveals anything reliable about personality.

construct_options.text: The study of handwriting, often as a supposed guide to personality
The study of graphs and statistical charts
The study of ancient written languages

context_sentences: She moved into gerontology after realising how few doctors specialised in the needs of older patients.
The clinic’s gerontology unit had grown to become its busiest department within a decade.

construct_options.text: The scientific study of ageing and the problems of the elderly
The study of family trees and ancestry
The study of infants and early childhood development
